# The Logic of Scientific Discovery

*[Popper](../philosophers/popper.md) · 1934 · *Logik der Forschung**

Popper breaks with the idea that science proves its laws by piling up observations: no verification makes a law certain, but a single experiment can refute it. What marks something as a science is thus its falsifiability, its capacity to expose itself to disproof. Knowledge advances through bold conjectures and the elimination of errors.
